# Verkli

Verkli is a publishing platform for indie authors. Authors publish books in
multiple languages with optional audiobook and print-on-demand editions.
Readers discover and read books on the web.

This file follows the llms.txt convention. It is intended for AI agents that
want a fast, machine-readable map of the site.

## What you can do here

- Discover published books and their authors
- Read book metadata (title, description, language, price, formats)
- Follow links to canonical pages for human reading

You cannot purchase, write, or perform any account-level actions through the
public API. Those flows require a logged-in user in the web app.

## Important URLs

- Site root: https://verkli.com
- Reader home: https://verkli.com/reader
- Discover: https://verkli.com/reader/discover
- All authors: https://verkli.com/reader/authors
- Genres: https://verkli.com/reader/genres
- How it works: https://verkli.com/reader/how-it-works
- Sitemap: https://verkli.com/sitemap.xml
- Robots: https://verkli.com/robots.txt

## Public API (read-only)

Base: https://verkli.com/api/public

- GET /books — list published books (page, limit, q, language, is_free)
- GET /books/{id} — single book by UUID or slug
- GET /authors/{id} — public author profile + published books (UUID or username)
- GET /openapi.json — OpenAPI 3.1 schema for this API
- POST /mcp — Model Context Protocol endpoint (JSON-RPC 2.0,
  streamable HTTP). Tools: search_books, get_book, get_author.
  GET /mcp returns server info.

Rate limit: 60 requests per minute per IP.
No authentication required. No write endpoints.

## Structured data

Every published book and public author page includes JSON-LD:

- Books emit schema.org/Book with author, language, offers, image, url
- Authors emit schema.org/Person with name, description, sameAs, url

## Policies

- Drafts and unpublished books are never returned by the public API
- Private author profiles are returned only when they have at least one
  published book
- All payloads are stable: fields may be added, never removed without a
  deprecation period
